evidence: The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.
Career infrastructure, not career tool. Like a life operating system.
Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.
Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.
Information asymmetry is increasing, not decreasing. The ATS is a permanent ceiling, not a problem to solve.
Handshake is the actual perfect acquirer - existing product-market fit with career centers; Handshake has distribution to all colleges

Pivot to HIRING side: realized they've built a high-signal community. ~3-4K users, 1/3+ with multiple stories. Deep data unlike any other platform.

source_display: Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets, not a bug.
An Indian recruiter placing US engineers said: I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.

source_display: Response Rate as Only Metric That Matters
evidence: If Careerspan's candidates respond at higher rates than other sources, that's sufficient proof of value. Use response rates as the convincing proxy - our users pay attention to our recommendations.

source_display: Response Rate as Only Metric That Matters
evidence: Creates a strong indication of them actually being interested - validates response rate metrics and protects candidate agency

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Prop
evidence: What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters.

Superposition wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade. This is pure signaling economics—the brand IS the product.
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was essentially: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' This inverts the network effects playbook - we're valuable because we're exclusive.

Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution. Brand names provide website legitimacy; individual recruiters drive actual success stories.
